title: Mary J. Blige & Lauryn Hill Tribute — The Betty Atlanta
description: Atlanta pays tribute to two R&B icons. Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM Sunday May 31. Three-hour live music experience featuring performances celebrating Mary J. Blige and Lauryn Hill's greatest hits. Dress to impress — The Betty is a stylish Buckhead venue.
location: The Betty, 374 East Paces Ferry Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30305
vibe: Soulful, energetic, nostalgic — perfect for R&B lovers who want to dance and sing along

title: Full Moon Rooftop Party — St. Julep, Atlanta
description: Dance under the May 31st full moon at St. Julep's rooftop bar in Atlanta. The event runs 7–11 PM with live DJs, craft cocktails, and skyline views. Outdoor space makes it ideal for warmer nights. Goes until midnight — a great Sunday night date to end the weekend.
location: St. Julep, 1411 E Broadway Ave, Atlanta, GA 30306
vibe: Chill, romantic rooftop vibes — great for couples who love dancing and open-air settings

title: Amy Sherald: American Sublime — High Museum of Art
description: The largest exhibition ever from the Baltimore-born, Atlanta-based painter known for her luminous portraits of everyday Black life. 50+ works spanning her entire career. Runs through August so this weekend is the moment. Dress casual cool, walk the Peckitt Corner galleries after, grab coffee at the museum café.
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA 30309
vibe: Sophisticated, thought-provoking, conversation-starting — ideal for a daytime Saturday date
